Free Forever

What is portfolio overlap?

When you invest in multiple mutual funds, each fund independently buys a basket of stocks. Sometimes two or more funds buy the same stock — this is overlap.

Example: You invest ₹13L in Parag Parikh Flexi Cap AND ₹13L in ICICI Pru Nasdaq 100.
Both funds independently choose to buy Microsoft.
→ Your money is buying Microsoft twice — once via each fund.
→ The second purchase is the "duplicate exposure."

The duplicate exposure is not necessarily bad — the risk is concentration: if that stock falls sharply, multiple funds feel it simultaneously. Run the analysis below to see exactly how much of your money is buying the same stocks twice.

Enter each fund's name, your allocation %, and upload its monthly portfolio disclosure (Excel format from AMC website). Add as many funds as you like.

Step 02
Your Overlap Analysis

Capital Allocation — Unique vs Duplicate

Every rupee you invest is either going into a unique stock (held by only one fund) or a duplicate stock (held by two or more funds). This bar shows the split.
Unique exposure (non-overlapping)
Duplicate exposure (overlapping)
Is your overlap good or bad?
Portfolio TypeTypical OverlapVerdict
Two large cap funds (same category)50–60%Poor
Three funds, same AMC40–55%Poor
Well-diversified 4-fund portfolio25–35%Average
Diversified 5–10 fund portfolio15–22%Good
Your portfolio——
Theoretical perfect diversification0%Ideal

Most retail investors with 5–7 funds unknowingly carry 40–50% overlap. Below 20% with multiple funds represents genuinely good diversification.

Fund Pair Overlap — Visual

Each diagram shows the weight overlap between two funds (as % of fund weights, not capital-weighted). Larger intersection = more similar stock selection by fund managers.

Pairwise Overlap Matrix

Capital-weighted overlap between each pair of funds. Shows how much of your ₹ investment in both funds combined is buying the same stocks.

How is the duplicate amount calculated?

For each stock held by two or more funds, we find your portfolio-level exposure through each fund, then take the minimum as the duplicate — the smaller purchase is the "second buy."

Why capital & allocation weighted? Other tools show higher numbers.

Most Indian overlap tools use simpler methods — neither accounts for your personal allocation split.

This tool is for educational purposes only. It does not constitute investment advice. Data is based on uploaded AMC monthly portfolio disclosure. Results depend on the accuracy and recency of uploaded data.

Works with monthly portfolio disclosure (Excel) from PPFAS, ICICI Prudential, Helios, Kotak, Nippon, Motilal Oswal, HDFC, Axis, SBI, Mirae and most Indian AMCs.
